phases.name: Read the book
phases.plain: Read the source — whatever form it arrives in. PDFs go through Azure Document Intelligence: every page recognised, Arabic script preserved, footnotes separated. Audio lectures go through Turboscribe in native-language mode, then Azure Translator with a tradition-specific glossary so technical terms like ta'wil and haqaiq reach the pipeline intact. Mixed sources run both paths and merge before the next station.

phases.name: Strip the noise
phases.plain: Every source carries baggage — historical background, chains of narration, scholarly apparatus, lecture filler. A model-driven routing layer strips it intelligently: Haiku handles obvious structural patterns, Sonnet reviews ambiguous passages, Gemini handles tradition-specific idioms it knows well. Nothing is fixed-pattern. What survives is pure teaching content. A noise log is written so you can audit what was removed.
phases.model_routing: Haiku → obvious | Sonnet → ambiguous | Gemini → tradition-specific

phases.name: Polish the text
phases.plain: Clean and normalize the stripped text — fix OCR artefacts, align sentence breaks, standardize transliterations. Arabic-origin technical terms are preserved verbatim; the pipeline never attempts to translate ta'wil or haqaiq into paraphrases.

phases.name: Learn the names
phases.plain: Every preserved Arabic and Urdu term gets a pronunciation guide — mandatory for any book with Ismaili or Sufi vocabulary. The phonetics sidecar travels with every episode bundle so the narrator never guesses. Pre-gate analysis runs here too, checking that each chapter has a teachable core before the review gate opens.

phases.name: Source Review Gate
phases.plain: The pipeline halts. The astro site shows you every chapter, the noise-stripped log, vocabulary coverage warnings, and any structural gaps flagged by the pre-gate analysis. You read, edit source files directly in podcast-reader, come back and approve. Only when all warnings clear — or you override them with a reason — does the pipeline continue. No expensive phases run on content you haven't vetted.

phases.name: Plan the episodes
phases.plain: Source chapters are pooled into a single de-structured corpus — chapter and section boundaries from the original are discarded. The blueprint agent classifies the book and re-slices the content into ~30-minute episodes governed by learning arc, not source organisation. Each episode must close a teaching loop: hook → core teaching → worked example → application → bridge to next episode.

phases.name: Enrich with context
phases.plain: For each episode, gather supporting context the narrator will need. A tradition firewall governs what enters: Ismaili books only receive Ismaili-tradition atoms; Sunni books receive Sunni atoms; universal atoms (shared across traditions) are always available. The augmenter never injects content from a conflicting tradition without an explicit opt-in framing note.

phases.name: Bring in outside knowledge
phases.plain: The knowledge base — Quranic verses, hadith, and doctrinal atoms, all tagged by tradition — is queried for each episode. Missing citations are flagged and added to the knowledge base when verified, compounding accuracy across every future book. Incomplete books are processed from what exists; the intelligence layer supplements depth, never invents missing content.

phases.name: Cut the chapter pieces
phases.plain: Slice the approved, enriched content into self-contained bundles — one per episode — ready for NotebookLM upload. Each bundle carries the source text, pronunciation sidecar, and enrichment notes as a single package.

phases.name: Write the narrator framing
phases.plain: Write the Customize-box briefing for each episode — the short instruction that tells the two AI hosts what to discuss, what to emphasise, what to avoid, and which host leads. Host roles are assigned consistently across the series; they never flip between episodes.

phases.name: Optimize for teaching
phases.plain: Claude Sonnet reviews each authored chapter before the auditor sees it. Three checks: host-role consistency (roles never flip mid-series), teaching arc validation (hook → teaching → example → application → bridge), and NotebookLM format hygiene (no markdown artefacts, no over-long monologue paragraphs, no heading-driven narration). Sonnet is used — not Gemini — so Gemini remains a genuinely independent auditor in the next phase.

phases.name: Design the slides
phases.plain: Author the visual deck for each episode — restating an idea, illustrating a passage literally, surfacing the structure with a diagram. Slides complement the audio without duplicating it.

phases.name: Audit and converge
phases.plain: Two independent reviewers — Claude Challenger and Gemini second-opinion — read every bundle and framing. Gemini's independence is preserved because it did not touch the content in P7g. Loops until the book passes or a finding needs a human call. Up to five passes per chapter, fifteen across the full cycle.

phases.name: Publish Review Gate
phases.plain: The pipeline halts a second time. The same astro site view now shows the fully authored product: episode list, challenger findings, optimization summary, and the NotebookLM upload checklist. You cannot edit source here — changes go back to source files and re-run from the affected phase. When you approve, the book moves to the published catalog and the working branch merges back.

agents.name: Html View Challenger
agents.role: Conformance challenger for HTML views, Astro pages, components, and diagrams on
agents.plain: Conformance challenger for HTML views, Astro pages, components, and diagrams on the Podcast Factory Astro Site (directory plan-dashboard/). Validates a built view against the Cortex HTML View Quality Standard (the 74 REQ-NNN rules in _wo…

agents.name: Podcast Auditor
agents.plain: Repo-level health audit for podcast-factory. Surfaces drift, regressions, gaps, and refactor opportunities across the operational surface (pipeline scripts, skill files, agent specs, plan docs). Identify-only in v1.0. Produces a structur…

agents.name: The Episode Planner
agents.plain: Reads the polished book, classifies what kind of book it is, and decides how the chapters should map to episodes — which to merge, which to split, which to drop. The output is the episode list you approve at the one human checkpoint.
agents.what_it_knows: Five planning modes — tribunal arc, chronological, problem-solution, vignette grid, dialectical pairs. The archetype registry. The narrative-density signal that decides which mode to use.
agents.boundary_in: A polished book and the genre/audience defaults from the book config.
agents.boundary_out: A classification report and an episode-plan draft (titles, word-count targets, in series order).
agents.does_not: Cross book boundaries (one book at a time). Override the human's edits to the plan during the approval halt.
agents.failure_mode: If classification confidence is low, falls back to chronological mode and flags the classification for human review.

agents.name: The Quality Reviewer
agents.plain: Reads every episode bundle and every narrator framing the way a careful editor would. Catches citation errors, broken phonetics, accidental clichés, framing that contradicts the source, and anything else the static checks miss. Loops until the work converges — up to five passes per chapter, fifteen across the full convergence cycle.
agents.what_it_knows: The full doctrinal rule registry, the anti-cliché registry, the source bundle for the chapter under review, every prior finding on this book.
agents.boundary_in: A draft chapter bundle plus its framing.
agents.boundary_out: A ship-ready, ship-with-caution, or needs-rewrite verdict with severity-ranked findings.
agents.does_not: Edit the spec or the doctrine (the trainer does that). Block on issues the framing author already justified.
agents.failure_mode: After three convergence failures on the same finding, escalates to human review rather than spinning further.

agents.name: Podcast Extract
agents.role: Narrow orchestrator for the single-chapter → NotebookLM bundle path
agents.plain: Narrow orchestrator for the single-chapter → NotebookLM bundle path. Resolves a chapter reference, ensures its contract exists (scaffolds a stub if absent), invokes scripts/podcast/extract_chapter.py, and returns the emitted bundle paths…

agents.name: Podcast Librarian
agents.role: Knowledge-extraction and dedup agent for phase 0h-knowledge-extract
agents.plain: Knowledge-extraction and dedup agent for phase 0h-knowledge-extract. Reads enriched chapters from a book at the end of phase 08-enrichment, extracts atoms (Wave 1 = Quran verses + hadith only), and merges them into the canonical knowledg…

agents.name: The Pipeline Driver
agents.plain: The conductor. Walks a book from PDF intake all the way through to the published catalog, handing off to each station in turn and recovering from anything that breaks along the way. Hands-off for hours at a time — there is exactly one human checkpoint in its entire run, at the episode-plan review halt.
agents.what_it_knows: The full pipeline order, the cost ledger, the watchdog protocol, the orchestrator state file format, every other agent's invocation contract.
agents.boundary_in: A PDF in the inbox plus a one-line approval after the episode plan halts.
agents.boundary_out: A merged develop branch with the book in the published catalog and a per-book cost report.
agents.does_not: Validate quality (the challenger does that). Edit the design rules (the trainer does that). Skip the human checkpoint at episode-plan review.
agents.cost_profile: $25–$50 per book end to end, depending on length tier.
agents.failure_mode: Halts and surfaces the exact phase that failed. The watchdog separately detects hangs and force-restarts.

agents.name: The Planner
agents.plain: Two modes. Guardian mode reviews every proposed change to the plan files for regression risk — against architectural invariants, frozen shipped-book state, in-flight book interference, and brittleness anti-patterns — and pushes back in plain English before anything is written. Builder mode picks up an approved roadmap step and executes it end to end, running its own guardian self-check before touching a line.
agents.what_it_knows: The full architecture spec, the 23-step roadmap, the debt ledger, the cost ledger, the design protocol that governs every visible string on this dashboard.
agents.boundary_in: Either a proposed plan-file edit or an approved roadmap step ID.
agents.boundary_out: A verdict (Guardian) or a completed step plus a regenerated dashboard (Builder). Always regenerates the three snapshot files as a mandatory side-effect.
agents.does_not: Edit shipped-book content. Skip its own guardian self-check in Builder mode. Touch the sibling journal repo.
agents.cost_profile: $0.50–$2 per audit. $5–$25 per implementation step depending on scope.
agents.failure_mode: Blocks the change with a per-check breakdown and a concrete suggested rewrite.

agents.name: The Publisher
agents.plain: The thin agent that moves a finished book from the workshop into the public catalog. Refuses to publish unless seven readiness gates all pass — structure, episode pairing, sequential numbering, build cleanliness, state shippability, target wipe-safety, convergence verdict.
agents.what_it_knows: The seven gate checks. The catalog file layout. The destination wipe-safety rules.
agents.boundary_in: A book whose convergence loop has reported ship-ready or ship-with-caution on every chapter.
agents.boundary_out: A book moved from the drafts tree to the published tree, with a merge-ready branch.
agents.does_not: Publish anything in non-orchestrated mode without an explicit override flag. Re-publish a book that's already in the catalog.
agents.cost_profile: Effectively free — file moves and gate checks only.
agents.failure_mode: Halts on the first failing gate and surfaces the specific gate.

agents.name: Podcast Trainer
agents.role: Cross-book pattern learner for the /podcast skill — proposes targeted refinement
agents.plain: Cross-book pattern learner for the /podcast skill — proposes targeted refinements to the podcast-challenger spec and podcast handbook based on patterns found in shipped book branches, but ONLY commits changes that pass a held-out regress…

agents.name: Postprod Review
agents.role: Post-production audit for NotebookLM-generated audio episodes
agents.plain: Post-production audit for NotebookLM-generated audio episodes. Consumes Turboscribe-produced transcripts of the downloaded m4a files and scores each against (a) the book's genre archetype and (b) the Dos/Don'ts protocol. Identifies drift…

agents.name: Project Steward
agents.plain: Project-stewardship agent. Explicitly invoked via `/steward <scope>` — NOT autonomous. Plans and prioritizes work for the podcast-factory project by composing existing agents (`repo-surgeon`, `podcast-challenger`, `reconcile`, `podcast-a…

agents.name: Refine Prompt
agents.role: Refines a raw user request into a single compact instruction-paragraph tuned for
agents.plain: Refines a raw user request into a single compact instruction-paragraph tuned for Claude Opus 4.7 / Claude Code (VS Code) in the journal repo. Inventories the repo for anti-regression hints, distills the Operating Contract inline, and emi…

agents.name: Repo Surgeon
agents.role: Holistic repo architecture reviewer, regression hunter, plan-conformance auditor
agents.plain: Holistic repo architecture reviewer, regression hunter, plan-conformance auditor, and cleanup enforcer. Runs five passes — Structure, Code, Architecture, Brittleness, Plan Conformance — then generates a repair plan and executes approved …

agents.name: The Slide Reviewer
agents.plain: The visual counterpart of the quality reviewer. Verifies that each slide either restates an idea cleanly, illustrates a passage literally, or surfaces structure with a useful diagram — and that the deck as a whole does not just echo the audio.
agents.what_it_knows: The deck-structure ladder, the visual-density threshold, the diagram-type discipline rules, the just-skip protocol.
agents.boundary_in: A draft slide bundle plus the corresponding chapter framing.
agents.boundary_out: A per-chapter verdict plus per-slide notes the worker re-authors against.
agents.does_not: Author the slides itself (a separate worker does the authoring). Force a deck when visual density is genuinely low.
agents.failure_mode: Surfaces a justified-skip verdict on low-density chapters instead of forcing slide creation.

agents.name: Vacuum
agents.role: Pipeline-aware folder hygiene and filename normalizer for podcast-factory books
agents.plain: Pipeline-aware folder hygiene and filename normalizer for podcast-factory books. Executes targeted mutations against `content/drafts/<slug>/` and `content/published/books/<slug>/` — renames audio/transcript files to canonical `ch<NN>-<sl…

modules.name: Book-kind recognizer
modules.plain: A small registry that knows the rules for each kind of book — a play-novel reads differently than a scholarly treatise. Adding support for a new kind of book means adding one entry here, never rewriting the pipeline.

modules.name: House style guard
modules.plain: A collection of constraints that apply across every book — how the Imam is referred to, what tone the narrators adopt, what topics are out of bounds. Enforced at every station.

modules.name: Cliché blocker
modules.plain: A growing list of phrases and openings the system has learned to avoid — banal welcomes, overused metaphors, podcast clichés that flatten the source's voice.

modules.name: Shared context
modules.plain: The single contract through which all background context flows into the narrator framings. One place to change what context the system passes around.

modules.name: Pronunciation guide
modules.plain: A per-book mapping of every name and term in the book to its phonetic spelling — so the narrator says "Ibn al-Walid" the way the source intends, not the way English spelling suggests.

modules.name: Outside-knowledge gatherer
modules.plain: Pulls modern research and cross-references that strengthen an episode without distorting the source — used carefully, off by default for sensitive books.

modules.name: Quality reviewer
modules.plain: Reads every episode bundle and every framing the way a careful editor would, flags problems by severity, and pushes them back for correction. Loops until convergence.

modules.name: Slide deck reviewer
modules.plain: The visual counterpart of the quality reviewer — checks every slide for restatement, literal illustration, structural value, and audio-deck divergence.

layers.name: Foundation
layers.plain: Files everything else depends on: paths, the database, the rule registry, branch naming.

layers.name: House style
layers.plain: Doctrinal rules, the archetype registry, the cost ledger, the shared context contract.

layers.name: Intelligence
layers.plain: The extractor, the librarian, the augmenter — the parts that read, remember, and enrich.

layers.name: Authoring
layers.plain: The pieces that actually write framings, capstones, prefaces, augmentations.

layers.name: Stations
layers.plain: One file per pipeline station. Thin orchestration that calls the layers below.

layers.name: Driver
layers.plain: A single thin driver that walks the station order. Hands off to each station in turn.

adrs.title: SQLite first, Postgres later
adrs.plain: Use a single-file database for now; defer the heavier setup until we actually need it.

adrs.title: Tier-2 capstone NEVER reads chapter-scope material
adrs.plain: Tier-2 capstone NEVER reads chapter-scope material

adrs.title: No file over six hundred lines
adrs.plain: Hard line cap to keep modules small and readable. Triggers refactor when crossed.

adrs.title: Augmenter default-disabled until G12 fires green
adrs.plain: Augmenter default-disabled until G12 fires green

adrs.title: Per-book scratch stays JSONL, not SQLite
adrs.plain: Per-book scratch stays JSONL, not SQLite

adrs.title: No file version stamps
adrs.plain: Every file IS the current version. Git history is the version log. Pre-commit hook enforces.

adrs.title: Astro for the plan SPA + design tokens shared with podcast-reader
adrs.plain: Astro for the plan SPA + design tokens shared with podcast-reader

adrs.title: Shipped books are frozen
adrs.plain: Five books are already shipped. New improvements ship as addendum episodes, not as re-runs of the original.

adrs.title: Strategic-tactical agent split: steward composes, doesn't reimplement
adrs.plain: Strategic-tactical agent split: steward composes, doesn't reimplement

adrs.title: Annotation output must persist as both queue and chapter handoff file
adrs.plain: Annotation output must persist as both queue and chapter handoff file
